quiz_name: Cost Management Budget and Cost Baseline

questions.stem: What is the purpose of the Determine Budget process?
choices: Aggregate cost estimates into a project budget
questions.explanation: Determine Budget combines individual activity and work package estimates into the total project budget.

choices: A time phased project budget used to measure cost performance
questions.explanation: The cost baseline is the approved budget distributed across the timeline of the project.
BAC represents the total approved budget for the entire project.

questions.stem: Which document organizes project work in a hierarchy and supports cost estimation?
questions.explanation: The WBS breaks project work into manageable components that can be estimated.

questions.stem: Why are reserves included in project budgets?
choices: To account for risk and uncertainty
questions.explanation: Reserves account for known and unknown risks affecting cost.

questions.stem: What is the main difference between cost estimates and the cost baseline?
choices: Estimates predict costs while the baseline is the approved budget
Estimates are used only by finance teams
questions.explanation: Cost estimates predict costs while the cost baseline is the approved version used for performance measurement.

questions.stem: Which input helps determine the overall project budget?
questions.explanation: Cost estimates from work packages are aggregated to form the project budget.

questions.stem: Which concept represents funding provided in increments over time?
questions.explanation: Funding requirements describe how funds are distributed during the project lifecycle.
